label: SBLJ_label
reason: From ~2148–2156, Mario performs repeated long-jump frames while angled sideways against the red staircase wall in the castle lobby. His screen-space speed increases from ~150 px/s to ~650 px/s over ~0.27 s (≈8 frames), indicating acceleration consistent with step-snap accumulation from a Side BLJ. Between 2156→2158 (0.067 s) he abruptly teleports from the stairs into the underfloor black void (BRoD), which cannot occur with normal physics. This sudden OOB warp after sideways long jumps on steps matches an SBLJ-induced clip.

label: LBLJ_label
reason: For ~0.8–1.0 s Mario stays nearly stationary beside the lobby green pillars while repeatedly entering the crouched long‑jump pose (building backwards speed). Then within ~4–5 frames (~0.13–0.17 s) he rockets across ~5–6 checker tiles toward the star door—an average speed >3× normal long‑jump speed—and there is no door‑open animation. The next frames show Mario inside a black void room behind the door, indicating he clipped through it using hyperspeed. This is the lobby BLJ used to pass the 8‑star door.

label: Normal
reason: Mario jumps into the star panel (Secret Slide), transitions normally, runs the entry hallway and long-jumps onto the slide. Estimated screen‑space speeds are smooth and plausible: ~200–300 px/s while approaching the panel, increasing gradually to ~400–600 px/s when entering the slide with steady positive acceleration (no sudden spikes). There is no backward movement, no repeated long jumps against stairs/pillars, and no clipping through doors/walls. The motion profile matches normal jump/long‑jump and slide acceleration, so none of the listed glitches occur.

label: Normal
reason: Across ~1.34 s (≈30 fps), Mario runs right and jumps once onto/under a moving platform, then grabs a pole. Per-frame horizontal displacement is small and smooth (≈10–20 px/frame, ~300–600 px/s) with no sudden spikes; the vertical motion is a normal parabolic jump with an apex after ~0.4 s and no instant position snaps. When the camera tilts up to the underside of the orange platform, Mario re-enters the view and grabs the pole gradually; there is no single-frame warp from the pole base to the mesh above that would indicate a Pole Glitch, nor any backwards acceleration, stair snapping, or wall/door clipping consistent with BLJ/SBLJ or other listed glitches. Movement and state changes match standard gameplay.

label: Normal
reason: Over the 41 frames (~1.34 s), Mario emerges from a hole, runs across grates, makes standard jumps, and climbs a slanted yellow fence. Motion is smooth and always forward-facing with no sudden teleports or clipping. Example estimates: frames ~15–20 show ~120 px horizontal travel in ~0.17 s (~700 px/s); frames ~28–34 show ~200 px in ~0.33 s (~600 px/s). A jump to the fence rises ~70–80 px over ~0.23 s (gravity-consistent arc). No evidence of negative hyperspeed, rapid step-snapping, or abnormal acceleration associated with BLJ/SBLJ/LBLJ or any listed clips (no poles, doors, Bob-ombs, or wall passes used). This sequence reflects normal platforming.

label: Miscellaneous_wall_clipping_label
reason: Across ~1.34 s (≈41 frames), Mario runs at normal ground speed (~6–8 px/frame ≈180–240 px/s), then performs a normal-looking triple-jump with smooth vertical deceleration (apex reached after ~15–18 frames). Around frames 8158–8161 he intersects the ceiling: within 2–3 frames he transitions from just below the ceiling to fully inside/behind the ceiling geometry, with an upward displacement >20–30 px in a single frame (>600–900 px/s), not preceded by BLJ-style backwards acceleration or object interaction. The camera immediately clips into the ceiling, indicating a precise seam/ceiling pass-through without hyperspeed. This matches a non-hyperspeed wall/ceiling seam clip, i.e., Miscellaneous Wall Clipping.

label: Normal
reason: Across the ~1.34 s (41 frames), Mario walks/runs up the red stairs and triggers the normal star-door opening. Early frames show a modest, steady advance up several steps (~60–100 px over ~0.3 s ≈ 200–330 px/s), then his motion effectively stops as the door opening animation plays (camera pushes forward while the door visibly swings on its hinge). There are no backward long-jump crouch frames, no rapid backward step-snapping, no sudden multi-step teleports, and no clipping through a closed door. The observed velocities are consistent with normal movement and a standard door entry, not BLJ/LBLJ/SBLJ or DDD skip.

label: BLJ_label
reason: Across the 41-frame (~1.34 s) sequence Mario is on the red castle staircase facing the camera and repeatedly entering the long‑jump crouch while moving backwards up the steps. For roughly the first ~20 frames his displacement per frame is small (≈5–10 px/frame), then between ~frames 8818–8821 he suddenly rockets up the stairs: the screen scrolls an entire staircase length (>300 px) in ~0.1 s (>3000 px/s), far exceeding normal long‑jump speed (forward long jumps cap at ~48 u/f). This rapid increase in backward velocity and the immediate transition to the Bowser painting room is characteristic of a Backwards Long Jump that abuses the lack of a backward speed cap to gain large negative speed. No other listed glitches are needed to explain the motion; the brief pillar contact later is just camera overlap.
